29.reason: [Error: The return value of add is not assigned to root->lch, so the new node is not linked to the parent. Should be: root->lch = add(root->lch, x);]

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-1: 0.5 , reason:  [Error: The return value of add is not assigned to root->lch, so the new node is not linked to the parent. Should be: root->lch = add(root->lch, x);]
reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-2: 0.5 , reason:  [Error: The return value of add is not assigned to root->lch, so the new node is not linked to the parent. Should be: root->lch = add(root->lch, x);]
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.5 , reason:  The recursive call returns a pointer but does not update the parent's left child; should be: root->lch = add(root->lch, x); then return root;
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 0.5 , reason:  The recursive call does not update the left child pointer of the current node; the new node is never attached to the tree.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.2 , reason:  Logical error: The return value of the recursive call is not assigned to root->lch, so the left child pointer is never updated, breaking the BST structure.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.5 , reason:  The return value of the recursive call is not assigned to root->lch, so the newly created node is never linked to the parent. It should be root->lch = add(root->lch, x);

32.reason: [Error: Same issue as above; should be: root->rch = add(root->rch, x);]

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-1: 0.5 , reason:  [Error: Same issue as above; should be: root->rch = add(root->rch, x);]
reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-2: 0.5 , reason:  [Error: Same issue as above; should be: root->rch = add(root->rch, x);]
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.5 , reason:  The recursive call returns a pointer but does not update the parent's right child; should be: root->rch = add(root->rch, x); then return root;
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 0.5 , reason:  The recursive call does not update the right child pointer of the current node; the new node is never attached to the tree.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.2 , reason:  Logical error: The return value of the recursive call is not assigned to root->rch, so the right child pointer is never updated, breaking the BST structure.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.5 , reason:  The return value of the recursive call is not assigned to root->rch, so the newly created node is never linked to the parent. It should be root->rch = add(root->rch, x);

38.reason: Potential null pointer dereference if root is NULL (e.g., when n=0); should verify root != NULL before accessing its members.
